purpose: Generate deep, commercially-focused discovery questions that surface how the business wins, loses, and delivers value

rules.core_principle: Every question must reveal commercial truth, not operational maturity
must_do: force comparison (win vs loss, strong vs weak, best vs rest)
expose misalignment between sales, proposition, and delivery
link directly to revenue, value, or deal outcomes
must_not_do: ask generic capability or process questions
rules.anchor: The system must ask how the business wins, not how it operates

lenses.name: People
questions: Where do your strongest individuals outperform the rest in how they position and close deals, and what is missing elsewhere?
Where does internal misalignment between sales, solution, and delivery create a different story for the client?
Where does your team’s capability limit the type or quality of work you can win?

scale_1_5.1: Teams cannot consistently articulate value; deals rely on individuals and are unpredictable
scale_1_5.3: Core value can be explained, but inconsistently across teams and deal types
scale_1_5.5: Clear, consistent, and compelling value articulation across all roles and client segments

lenses.name: Operations
questions: Where does your delivery model constrain what you can confidently sell?
Where have recent deals exposed a gap between what was sold and what could actually be delivered?
What types of work consistently create friction once they move into delivery?

scale_1_5.1: Delivery frequently breaks what is sold; high friction and rework
scale_1_5.3: Most work is delivered, but with inconsistencies or hidden inefficiencies
scale_1_5.5: Delivery model fully supports what is sold, with predictable and scalable outcomes

lenses.name: Technology
questions: Where does your technology genuinely differentiate you in a deal, and where is it overstated?
Where would a competitor most easily expose a weakness in your technical capability?
What capabilities do clients assume you have that you don’t fully deliver today?

scale_1_5.1: Technology is unclear, inconsistent, or overpromised
scale_1_5.3: Technology supports most deals but is not a strong differentiator
scale_1_5.5: Technology is a clear, credible differentiator that consistently helps win work

lenses.name: Commercial
questions: Across recent wins and losses, what patterns explain why clients choose or reject you?
What are clients actually buying from you at the point of sale?
Where are you winning work that you should not be winning?

scale_1_5.1: Win/loss outcomes are unclear or inconsistent; weak understanding of why deals succeed or fail
scale_1_5.3: Some patterns are understood, but not consistently applied
scale_1_5.5: Clear, repeatable understanding of win/loss drivers and strong alignment to ICP

lenses.name: Risk/Compliance
questions: Where has risk or compliance directly limited your ability to pursue or close deals?
Where does risk sit implicitly in deals rather than being explicitly understood?
Where do compliance requirements shape or distort your proposition?

scale_1_5.1: Risk is reactive, unclear, and regularly impacts deals negatively
scale_1_5.3: Risk is managed, but not consistently embedded into decision-making
scale_1_5.5: Risk is clearly understood, proactively managed, and supports confident deal execution

lenses.name: Partners
questions: Where do partners strengthen your ability to win, and where do they introduce risk?
Where have partner capabilities directly influenced deal success or failure?
How aligned are your partners to your value proposition in live deals?

scale_1_5.1: Partners create inconsistency, risk, or weaken deals
scale_1_5.3: Partners support delivery but are not a strong differentiator
scale_1_5.5: Partners are fully aligned and actively strengthen your ability to win and deliver
